title: August Wilson: The Ground on Which I Stand | August Wilson in Seattle | American Masters | PBS
content: # August Wilson in Seattle. After his death in 2005, the Seattle Repertory Theatre and the Seattle Center in which it is located, wanted to honor the playwright. August Wilson: The Ground on Which I Stand -- The Essential Ingredients for an August Wilson Play. August Wilson: The Ground on Which I Stand -- August Wilson's Timeless Work. August Wilson: The Ground on Which I Stand -- August Wilson and African American Culture. August Wilson: The Ground on Which I Stand -- Costume Designs for August Wilson Plays. August Wilson: The Ground on Which I Stand -- August Wilson on the Dialogue in His Plays. August Wilson: The Ground on Which I Stand -- Filmmaker Sam Pollard on His August Wilson Documentary. August Wilson: The Ground on Which I Stand -- August Wilson: The Ground on Which I Stand, Panel Discussion. August Wilson: The Ground on Which I Stand -- August Wilson's Youth. August Wilson: The Ground on Which I Stand.

title: August Wilson: Did you know...? | Seattle Rep
content: Read on to learn some little-known facts about Wilson, his work, and his time at Seattle Rep.*. Wilson made his acting debut at Seattle Rep in 2003 with his one-man autobiographical play, *How I Learned What I Learned*. August Wilson in *How I Learned* *What I Learned* at Seattle Rep. After Wilson’s death, a public tribute to the playwright was held on February 13, 2006 at Seattle Rep. Selections of The American Century Cycle were performed by well-known actors, including Ruben Santiago-Hudson (director of *Jitney*), Rocky Carroll, Chic Street Man, Cynthia Jones, Keith Randolph Smith (*Jitney* cast member), and Charlayne Woodard. Did you know Seattle Rep is one of the few theaters in the nation to produce August Wilson’s full ten-play American Century Cycle? Image representing Learning about How I Learned What I Learned. Check out this How I Learned What I Learned behind-the-scenes Meet & Greet conversation about what went into the making of How I Learned..., the magic of August Wilson’s writing and poetry, and much more.
